rationale_for_baseline_tier: Common warts are benign and self-managed with topical salicylic acid, filing and stopping picking, with routine review/cryotherapy if needed (T0). Atypical/non-resolving lesions route to GP for biopsy; secondary infection routes to GP.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A lesion ulcerating, bleeding on its own, growing rapidly, or becoming darkly/irregularly coloured; a non-resolving lesion; or the surrounding skin becoming hot, red and swollen with a red streak/fever
escalation_edges.rationale: Atypical lesions need biopsy to exclude malignancy; secondary infection needs treatment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: See a doctor if a lump ulcerates, bleeds on its own, grows fast, becomes dark/irregular or won't clear — it should be checked and possibly biopsied. Get seen sooner if the skin becomes hot, red and swollen.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Reassure it's benign; give a malignancy safety-net and stop-picking advice.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: These are harmless warts. The key is to stop picking them (that's what spreads them), file the hard skin and apply the acid nightly, and wash your hands after. They clear over a couple of months; a GP can freeze stubborn ones.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care + GP review/cryotherapy in ~2 weeks; GP for atypical or infected lesions
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Routine — GP/urgent care if a lesion looks atypical or the skin becomes infected.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Verruca vulgaris (multiple, autoinoculated); malignancy/infection screened negative; salicylic acid + stop-picking; cryotherapy for refractory; biopsy if atypical/non-resolving.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring an atypical lesion without a malignancy/biopsy safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An SCC/amelanotic melanoma is mislabelled as a wart and diagnosis is delayed.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No stop-picking advice, so warts keep spreading.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED for benign common warts — unnecessary.
